title: pittosporum sylvaticum
intro: Pittosporum sylvaticum, also known as the forget-me-not tree or the New Zealand tobacco tree, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. The leaves and flowers of the pittosporum sylvaticum plant have been traditionally used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are said to provide various health benefits, including relief from coughs and colds, and improved digestion. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal preparations due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
